memoryArtifacts.headline: Cornelius Fisher, 1629 Baptism, at Denton, Norfolk, England, son of Anthony & ALES Unknown Fisher.
memoryArtifacts.description: Baptism of Cornelius Fisher, son of Anthony & "Ales" Unknown Fisher.  This bapt. & the bapt. of Nathaniel Fisher in the same parish about 10 miles from from Syleham, supports Anthony's wife's given name as Alice.

zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Alice Fisher於1592年出生在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆。她於大約1615年與Anthony Fisher在英國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1642年3月27日之後在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可迪德哈姆，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可迪德哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Fisher
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle English fis(sc)her(e) ‘fisherman’ (Old English fiscere). In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from many other languages, including German Fischer and its Slavic(ized) variant Fišer (see Fiser ), Dutch Visser , Hungarian Halász (see Halasz ), Italian Pescatore , Slovenian Ribič (see Ribic ), and Croatian Ribić or Ribar .

English: in a few cases, possibly a topographic name for someone who lived near a fish weir on a river, from Middle English fis(sc)hwere, fisshyar ‘fish weir’ (Old English fiscwer, fiscgear), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Fisher in North Mundham, Sussex.
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Bradáin ‘descendant of Bradán’, a personal name meaning ‘salmon’. See Braden .

variants.name: Fischer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Gerhard, Helmut, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Dieter, Franz, Ernst.

German, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle High German visch, German Fisch ‘fish’ + the agent suffix -er. This form of the surname is also established in some other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, and the Netherlands. In Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is (also) established in the Slavicized forms Fiszer (Polish) and Fišer (see Fiser ). Compare Fisher .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Alice Fisher was born in 1592, in Syleham, Suffolk, England. She married Anthony Fisher about 1615, in England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died after 27 March 1642, in Dedham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, and was buried in Dedham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Alice Fisherは1592年にイギリススフォルクシレハムで生まれた。約1615年にイギリスでAnthony Fisherと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1642年3月27日以後にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクデドハムで死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクデドハムにて埋葬された。

ko.Dedham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 데드함
ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Alice Fisher(은)는 1592년 영국 수폴크 실함에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1615년 영국에서 Anthony Fisher(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1642년 3월 27일 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 데드함에서 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 데드함에 묻혔다.

ru.Dedham, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colon: дедхам, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Alice Fisher родилась в 1592 г. в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Anthony Fisher около 1615 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 27 марта 1642 г. в местности дедхам, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика и была похоронена в местности дедхам, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
